Name: AActor
Description:
Actor is the base class for an Object that can be placed or spawned in a level.
  Actors may contain a collection of ActorComponents, which can be used to control how actors move, how they are rendered, etc.
  The other main function of an Actor is the replication of properties and function calls across the network during play.
 
  @see UActorComponent

Variables.Name: PrimaryActorTick
CustomTimeDilation
Variables.Description: Allow each actor to run at a different time speed. The DeltaTime for a frame is multiplied by the global TimeDilation (in WorldSettings) and this CustomTimeDilation for this actor's tick.

Variables.Name: bAllowBPReceiveTickEvent
Variables.Description: If true, bp tick will be called , otherwise skipped

Variables.Name: bHidden
Variables.Description:
Allows us to only see this Actor in the Editor, and not in the actual game.
	  @see SetActorHiddenInGame()

Variables.Name: bNetTemporary
Variables.Description: If true, when the actor is spawned it will be sent to the client but receive no further replication updates from the server afterwards.

Variables.Name: bNetStartup
Variables.Description: If true, this actor was loaded directly from the map, and for networking purposes can be addressed by its full path name

Variables.Name: bOnlyRelevantToOwner
Variables.Description: If true, this actor is only relevant to its owner. If this flag is changed during play, all non-owner channels would need to be explicitly closed.

Variables.Name: bOwningSpecificNetConsideration
Variables.Description: If true, this actor is considered for replication in an owning-specific semantics.

Variables.Name: bRegionBasedNetConsideration
Variables.Description: If true, this actor is considered for replication in region-based semantics.

Variables.Name: bMRegionBasedNetConsideration
Variables.Description: If true, this actor is considered for replication in Mregion-based semantics.

Variables.Name: bFastDistBasedNetRelevancy
Variables.Description: If true, this actor is checked for relevancy by fast distance-based calculation.

Variables.Name: bGroupBasedNetRelevancy
Variables.Description: If true, this actor is checked for relevancy by relevancy group first.

Variables.Name: bLazyNetReplication
Variables.Description: If true, this actor is only replicated by calling ForceNetUpdate.

Variables.Name: bClientSimulatedRelevancy
bAlwaysRelevant
Variables.Description: Always relevant for network (overrides bOnlyRelevantToOwner).

Variables.Name: bTearOff
Variables.Description:
If true, this actor is no longer replicated to new clients, and is "torn off" (becomes a ROLE_Authority) on clients to which it was being replicated.
	  @see TornOff()

Variables.Name: bExchangedRoles
Variables.Description:
Whether we have already exchanged RoleRemoteRole on the client, as when removing then re-adding a streaming level.
	  Causes all initialization to be performed again even though the actor may not have actually been reloaded.

Variables.Name: bNetLoadOnClient
Variables.Description: This actor will be loaded on network clients during map load

Variables.Name: bNetUseOwnerRelevancy
Variables.Description: If actor has valid Owner, call Owner's IsNetRelevantFor and GetNetPriority

Variables.Name: bBlockInput
Variables.Description: If true, all input on the stack below this actor will not be considered

Variables.Name: bCanBeBaseForCharacter
Variables.Description: If true, all input on the stack below this actor will not be considered

Variables.Name: bAllowTickBeforeBeginPlay
Variables.Description:
Whether we allow this Actor to tick before it receives the BeginPlay event.
	  Normally we don't tick actors until after BeginPlay; this setting allows this behavior to be overridden.
	  This Actor must be able to tick for this setting to be relevant.

Variables.Name: bReplicates
Variables.Description:
If true, this actor will replicate to remote machines
	  @see SetReplicates()

Variables.Name: RemoteRole
Variables.Description: Describes how much control the remote machine has over the actor.

Variables.Name: Owner
Variables.Description:
Owner of this Actor, used primarily for replication (bNetUseOwnerRelevancy & bOnlyRelevantToOwner) and visibility (PrimitiveComponent bOwnerNoSee and bOnlyOwnerSee)
	  @see SetOwner(), GetOwner()

Variables.Name: bReplicateMovement
bActorEnableCollision
Variables.Description:
Enables any collision on this actor.
	  @see SetActorEnableCollision(), GetActorEnableCollision()

Variables.Name: NetDriverName
Variables.Description: Used to specify the net driver to replicate on (NAME_None || NAME_GameNetDriver is the default net driver)

Variables.Name: ReplicatedMovement
Variables.Description: Used for replication of our RootComponent's position and velocity

Variables.Name: InitialLifeSpan
Variables.Description: How long this Actor lives before dying, 0=forever. Note this is the INITIAL value and should not be modified once play has begun.

Variables.Name: AttachmentReplication
Variables.Description:
Used for replicating attachment of this actor's RootComponent to another actor.
	  This is filled in via GatherCurrentMovement() when the RootComponent has an AttachParent.

Variables.Name: Role
Variables.Description: Describes how much control the local machine has over the actor.

Variables.Name: NetDormancy
Variables.Description: Dormancy setting for actor to take itself off of the replication list without being destroyed on clients.

Variables.Name: AutoReceiveInput
Variables.Description: Automatically registers this actor to receive input from a player.

Variables.Name: InputPriority
Variables.Description: The priority of this input component when pushed in to the stack.

Variables.Name: InputComponent
Variables.Description: Component that handles input for this actor, if input is enabled.

Variables.Name: NetCullDistanceSquared
Variables.Description: Square of the max distance from the client's viewpoint that this actor is relevant and will be replicated.

Variables.Name: NetCullFactorSquared
Variables.Description: NetCullDistanceSquared Factor for Connection

Variables.Name: NetTag
Variables.Description: Internal - used by UWorld::ServerTickClients()

Variables.Name: NetConsiderFrequency
Variables.Description: How often (per second) this actor enters consider list, should be greater than or equal to NetUpdateFrequency

Variables.Name: NetUpdateFrequency
Variables.Description: How often (per second) this actor will be checked for replication, used to determine NetUpdateTime

Variables.Name: MinNetUpdateFrequency
Variables.Description: Used to determine what rate to throttle down to when replicated properties are changing infrequently

Variables.Name: NetPriority
Variables.Description: Priority for this actor when checking for replication in a low bandwidth or saturated situation, higher priority means it is more likely to replicate

Variables.Name: bAutoDestroyWhenFinished
Variables.Description: If true then destroy self when "finished", meaning all relevant components report that they are done and no timelines or timers are in flight.

Variables.Name: bCanBeDamaged
Variables.Description:
Whether this actor can take damage. Must be true for damage events (e.g. ReceiveDamage()) to be called.
	  @see TakeDamage(), ReceiveDamage()

Variables.Name: bCanNotifyDamager
Variables.Description:
Whether this actor can Notify damager. Must be true for notify damager events (PreDamageOther) to be called.
	  @see TakeDamage(), PreDamageOther()

Variables.Name: bActorIsBeingDestroyed
Variables.Description: Set when actor is about to be deleted.

Variables.Name: bCollideWhenPlacing
Variables.Description: This actor collides with the world when placing in the editor, even if RootComponent collision is disabled. Does not affect spawning, @see SpawnCollisionHandlingMethod

Variables.Name: bFindCameraComponentWhenViewTarget
Variables.Description: If true, this actor should search for an owned camera component to view through when used as a view target.

Variables.Name: bRelevantForNetworkReplays
Variables.Description: If true, this actor will be replicated to network replays (default is true)

Variables.Name: bGenerateOverlapEventsDuringLevelStreaming
Variables.Description: If true, this actor will generate overlap events when spawned as part of level streaming. You might enable this is in the case where a streaming level loads around an actor and you want overlaps to trigger.

Variables.Name: bCanBeInCluster
Variables.Description: If true, this actor can be put inside of a GC Cluster to improve Garbage Collection performance

Variables.Name: bAllowReceiveTickEventOnDedicatedServer
Variables.Description:
If false, the Blueprint ReceiveTick() event will be disabled on dedicated servers.
	  @see AllowReceiveTickEventOnDedicatedServer()

Variables.Name: bActorSeamlessTraveled
Variables.Description: Indicates the actor was pulled through a seamless travel.

Variables.Name: bIgnoresOriginShifting
Variables.Description: Whether this actor should not be affected by world origin shifting.

Variables.Name: bEnableAutoLODGeneration
Variables.Description: If true, and if World setting has bEnableHierarchicalLOD equal to true, then it will generate LODActor from groups of clustered Actor

Variables.Name: SpawnCollisionHandlingMethod
Variables.Description: Controls how to handle spawning this actor in a situation where it's colliding with something else. "Default" means AlwaysSpawn here.

Variables.Name: Instigator
Variables.Description: Pawn responsible for damage caused by this actor.

Variables.Name: Children
Variables.Description: Array of Actors whose Owner is this actor

Variables.Name: RootComponent
Variables.Description: Collision primitive that defines the transform (location, rotation, scale) of this Actor.

Variables.Name: ControllingMatineeActors
Variables.Description: The matinee actors that control this actor.

Variables.Name: Layers
Variables.Description: Layer's the actor belongs to.  This is outside of the editoronly data to allow hiding of LD-specified layers at runtime for profiling.

Variables.Name: ParentComponent
Variables.Description: The UChildActorComponent that owns this Actor.

Variables.Name: Tags
Variables.Description: Array of tags that can be used for grouping and categorizing.

Variables.Name: BlueprintCreatedComponents
Variables.Description: Array of ActorComponents that are created by blueprints and serialized per-instance.

Variables.Name: InstanceComponents
Variables.Description: Array of ActorComponents that have been added by the user on a per-instance basis.

Variables.Name: bSkipNewDuplicateOwnedComponents
Variables.Description:
If you call CreateComponentFromTemplate on an actor which already owns a component with the same name, problem comes out
	  Optimized version will return the existing component, but not duplicate a new one, in this case, setting this switch to true is necessary

Variables.Name: bBlockLandscapeDeform
Variables.Description: If this actor will block any overlap deform.

Variables.Name: ExportActorInLevel
Variables.Description: 在编辑器获取level里面actor的位置和朝向, 通过命令行方式导出到一个lua表格. feishen, 20210406

Variables.Name: PivotOffset
Variables.Description: Local space pivot offset for the actor

Variables.Name: ParentComponentActor_DEPRECATED
Variables.Description: The Actor that owns the UChildActorComponent that owns this Actor.

Variables.Name: GroupActor
Variables.Description: The group this actor is a part of.

Variables.Name: SpriteScale
Variables.Description: The scale to apply to any billboard components in editor builds (happens in any WITH_EDITOR build, including non-cooked games).

Variables.Name: ActorLabel
Variables.Description:
The friendly name for this actor, displayed in the editor.  You should always use AActor::GetActorLabel() to access the actual label to display,
	  and call AActor::SetActorLabel() or FActorLabelUtilities::SetActorLabelUnique() to change the label.  Never set the label directly.

Variables.Name: FolderPath
Variables.Description: The folder path of this actor in the world (empty=root,  separated)

Variables.Name: bHiddenEd
Variables.Description: Whether this actor is hidden within the editor viewport.

Variables.Name: bEditable
Variables.Description: Whether the actor can be manipulated by editor operations.

Variables.Name: bListedInSceneOutliner
Variables.Description: Whether this actor should be listed in the scene outliner.

Variables.Name: bIsEditorPreviewActor
Variables.Description: True if this actor is the preview actor dragged out of the content browser

Variables.Name: bHiddenEdLayer
Variables.Description: Whether this actor is hidden by the layer browser.

Variables.Name: bHiddenEdTemporary
Variables.Description: Whether this actor is temporarily hidden within the editor; used for showhideetc functionality wo dirtying the actor.

Variables.Name: bHiddenEdLevel
Variables.Description: Whether this actor is hidden by the level browser.

Variables.Name: bLockLocation
Variables.Description: If true, prevents the actor from being moved in the editor viewport.

Variables.Name: HiddenEditorViews
Variables.Description: Bitflag to represent which views this actor is hidden in, via per-view layer visibility.

Functions.Name: OnRep_ReplicateMovement
Functions.Description: Called on client when updated bReplicateMovement value is received for this actor.

Functions.Name: TearOff
Functions.Description: Networking - Server - TearOff this actor to stop replication to clients. Will set bTearOff to true.

Functions.Name: SetReplicates
Functions.Description:
Set whether this actor replicates to network clients. When this actor is spawned on the server it will be sent to clients as well.
	  Properties flagged for replication will update on clients if they change on the server.
	  Internally changes the RemoteRole property and handles the cases where the actor needs to be added to the network actor list.

Params.Name: bInReplicates
Params.Description: Whether this Actor replicates to network clients.

Functions.Name: SetReplicateMovement
Functions.Description: Set whether this actor's movement replicates to network clients.

Params.Name: bInReplicateMovement
Params.Description: Whether this Actor's movement replicates to clients.

Functions.Name: GetLocalRole
Functions.Description: Returns how much control the local machine has over this actor.

Functions.Name: GetRemoteRole
Functions.Description: Returns how much control the remote machine has over this actor.

Functions.Name: OnRep_AttachmentReplication
Functions.Description: Called on client when updated AttachmentReplication value is received for this actor.

Functions.Name: OnRep_Instigator
Functions.Description: Called on clients when Instigator is replicated.

Functions.Name: EnableInput
Functions.Description: Pushes this actor on to the stack of input being handled by a PlayerController.

Params.Name: PlayerController
Params.Description: The PlayerController whose input events we want to receive.

Functions.Name: DisableInput
Functions.Description: Removes this actor from the stack of input being handled by a PlayerController.

Params.Name: PlayerController
Params.Description: The PlayerController whose input events we no longer want to receive. If null, this actor will stop receiving input from all PlayerControllers.

Functions.Name: GetInputAxisValue
Functions.Description: Gets the value of the input axis if input is enabled for this actor.

Functions.Name: GetInputAxisKeyValue
Functions.Description: Gets the value of the input axis key if input is enabled for this actor.

Functions.Name: GetInputVectorAxisValue
Functions.Description: Gets the value of the input axis key if input is enabled for this actor.

Functions.Name: GetInstigator
Functions.Description: Returns the instigator for this actor, or NULL if there is none.

Functions.Name: GetInstigatorController
Functions.Description: Returns the instigator's controller for this actor, or NULL if there is none.

Functions.Name: GetTransform
Functions.Description: Get the actor-to-world transform.

Return.Description: The transform that transforms from actor space to world space.

Functions.Name: K2_GetActorLocation
Functions.Description: Returns the location of the RootComponent of this Actor

Functions.Name: K2_SetActorLocation
Functions.Description: Move the Actor to the specified location.

Params.Name: NewLocation
Params.Description: The new location to move the Actor to.

Params.Name: bSweep
Params.Description: Whether we sweep to the destination location, triggering overlaps along the way and stopping short of the target if blocked by something.

Params.Name: SweepHitResult
Params.Description: The hit result from the move if swept.

Params.Name: bTeleport
Params.Description: Whether we teleport the physics state (if physics collision is enabled for this object).

Return.Description: Whether the location was successfully set (if not swept), or whether movement occurred at all (if swept).

Functions.Name: K2_GetActorRotation
Functions.Description: Returns rotation of the RootComponent of this Actor.

Functions.Name: GetActorForwardVector
Functions.Description: Get the forward (X) vector (length 1.0) from this Actor, in world space.

Functions.Name: GetActorUpVector
Functions.Description: Get the up (Z) vector (length 1.0) from this Actor, in world space.

Functions.Name: GetActorRightVector
Functions.Description: Get the right (Y) vector (length 1.0) from this Actor, in world space.

Functions.Name: GetActorBounds
Functions.Description: Returns the bounding box of all components that make up this Actor (excluding ChildActorComponents).

Params.Name: bOnlyCollidingComponents
Params.Description: If true, will only return the bounding box for components with collision enabled.

Functions.Name: K2_GetRootComponent
Functions.Description: Returns the RootComponent of this Actor

Functions.Name: GetVelocity
Functions.Description: Returns velocity (in cms (Unreal Unitssecond) of the rootcomponent if it is either using physics or has an associated MovementComponent

Functions.Name: K2_SetActorRotation
Functions.Description: Set the Actor's rotation instantly to the specified rotation.

Params.Name: NewRotation
Params.Description: The new rotation for the Actor.

Params.Name: bTeleportPhysics
Params.Description: Whether we teleport the physics state (if physics collision is enabled for this object).

Return.Description: Whether the rotation was successfully set.

Functions.Name: K2_SetActorLocationAndRotation
Functions.Description: Move the actor instantly to the specified location and rotation.

Params.Name: NewLocation
Params.Description: The new location to teleport the Actor to.

Params.Name: NewRotation
Params.Description: The new rotation for the Actor.

Params.Name: bSweep
Params.Description: Whether we sweep to the destination location, triggering overlaps along the way and stopping short of the target if blocked by something.

Params.Name: SweepHitResult
Params.Description: The hit result from the move if swept.

Params.Name: bTeleport
Params.Description: Whether we teleport the physics state (if physics collision is enabled for this object).

Return.Description: Whether the rotation was successfully set.

Functions.Name: SetActorScale3D
Functions.Description: Set the Actor's world-space scale.

Functions.Name: GetActorScale3D
Functions.Description: Returns the Actor's world-space scale.

Functions.Name: GetDistanceTo
Functions.Description: Returns the distance from this Actor to OtherActor.

Functions.Name: GetSquaredDistanceTo
Functions.Description: Returns the squared distance from this Actor to OtherActor.

Functions.Name: GetHorizontalDistanceTo
Functions.Description: Returns the distance from this Actor to OtherActor, ignoring Z.

Functions.Name: GetVerticalDistanceTo
Functions.Description: Returns the distance from this Actor to OtherActor, ignoring XY.

Functions.Name: GetDotProductTo
Functions.Description: Returns the dot product from this Actor to OtherActor. Returns -2.0 on failure. Returns 0.0 for coincidental actors.

Functions.Name: GetHorizontalDotProductTo
Functions.Description: Returns the dot product from this Actor to OtherActor, ignoring Z. Returns -2.0 on failure. Returns 0.0 for coincidental actors.

Functions.Name: K2_AddActorWorldOffset
Functions.Description: Adds a delta to the location of this actor in world space.

Params.Name: DeltaLocation
Params.Description: The change in location.

Params.Name: bSweep
Params.Description: Whether we sweep to the destination location, triggering overlaps along the way and stopping short of the target if blocked by something.

Params.Name: SweepHitResult
Params.Description: The hit result from the move if swept.

Params.Name: bTeleport
Params.Description: Whether we teleport the physics state (if physics collision is enabled for this object).

Functions.Name: K2_AddActorWorldRotation
Functions.Description: Adds a delta to the rotation of this actor in world space.

Params.Name: DeltaRotation
Params.Description: The change in rotation.

Params.Name: bSweep
Params.Description: Whether to sweep to the target rotation (not currently supported for rotation).

Params.Name: SweepHitResult
Params.Description: The hit result from the move if swept.

Params.Name: bTeleport
Params.Description: Whether we teleport the physics state (if physics collision is enabled for this object).

Functions.Name: K2_AddActorWorldTransform
Functions.Description: Adds a delta to the transform of this actor in world space. Scale is unchanged.

Functions.Name: K2_SetActorTransform
Functions.Description: Set the Actors transform to the specified one.

Params.Name: NewTransform
Params.Description: The new transform.

Params.Name: bSweep
Params.Description: Whether we sweep to the destination location, triggering overlaps along the way and stopping short of the target if blocked by something.

Params.Name: bTeleport
Params.Description: Whether we teleport the physics state (if physics collision is enabled for this object).

Functions.Name: K2_AddActorLocalOffset
Functions.Description: Adds a delta to the location of this component in its local reference frame.

Params.Name: bSweep
Params.Description: Whether we sweep to the destination location, triggering overlaps along the way and stopping short of the target if blocked by something.

Params.Name: bTeleport
Params.Description: Whether we teleport the physics state (if physics collision is enabled for this object).

Functions.Name: K2_AddActorLocalRotation
Functions.Description: Adds a delta to the rotation of this component in its local reference frame

Params.Name: DeltaRotation
Params.Description: The change in rotation in local space.

Params.Name: bSweep
Params.Description: Whether we sweep to the destination location, triggering overlaps along the way and stopping short of the target if blocked by something.

Params.Name: bTeleport
Params.Description: Whether we teleport the physics state (if physics collision is enabled for this object).

Functions.Name: K2_AddActorLocalTransform
Functions.Description: Adds a delta to the transform of this component in its local reference frame

Params.Name: NewTransform
Params.Description: The change in transform in local space.

Params.Name: bSweep
Params.Description: Whether we sweep to the destination location, triggering overlaps along the way and stopping short of the target if blocked by something.

Params.Name: bTeleport
Params.Description: Whether we teleport the physics state (if physics collision is enabled for this object).

Functions.Name: K2_SetActorRelativeLocation
Functions.Description: Set the actor's RootComponent to the specified relative location.

Params.Name: NewRelativeLocation
Params.Description: New relative location of the actor's root component

Params.Name: bSweep
Params.Description: Whether we sweep to the destination location, triggering overlaps along the way and stopping short of the target if blocked by something.

Params.Name: bTeleport
Params.Description: Whether we teleport the physics state (if physics collision is enabled for this object).

Functions.Name: K2_SetActorRelativeRotation
Functions.Description: Set the actor's RootComponent to the specified relative rotation

Params.Name: NewRelativeRotation
Params.Description: New relative rotation of the actor's root component

Params.Name: bSweep
Params.Description: Whether we sweep to the destination location, triggering overlaps along the way and stopping short of the target if blocked by something.

Params.Name: bTeleport
Params.Description: Whether we teleport the physics state (if physics collision is enabled for this object).

Functions.Name: K2_SetActorRelativeTransform
Functions.Description: Set the actor's RootComponent to the specified relative transform

Params.Name: NewRelativeTransform
Params.Description: New relative transform of the actor's root component

Params.Name: bSweep
Params.Description: Whether we sweep to the destination location, triggering overlaps along the way and stopping short of the target if blocked by something.

Params.Name: bTeleport
Params.Description: Whether we teleport the physics state (if physics collision is enabled for this object).

Functions.Name: SetActorRelativeScale3D
Functions.Description: Set the actor's RootComponent to the specified relative scale 3d

Params.Name: NewRelativeScale
Params.Description: New scale to set the actor's RootComponent to

Functions.Name: GetActorRelativeScale3D
Functions.Description: Return the actor's relative scale 3d

Functions.Name: SetActorHiddenInGame
Functions.Description: Sets the actor to be hidden in the game

Params.Name: bNewHidden
Params.Description: Whether or not to hide the actor and all its components

Functions.Name: OnActorSimulatedRelevant
Functions.Description: NOTE : Callback of Check Actor Relevancy in Client for ob or replay

Params.Name: bIsRelevant
Params.Description: : Whether or not relevant for replay view target

Params.Name: bConsiderHidden
Params.Description: : Whether or not to hide the actor and all its components

Functions.Name: SetActorEnableCollision
Functions.Description: Allows enablingdisabling collision for the whole actor

Functions.Name: GetActorEnableCollision
Functions.Description: Get current state of collision for the whole actor

Functions.Name: K2_DestroyActor
Functions.Description: Destroy the actor

Functions.Name: HasAuthority
Functions.Description: Returns whether this actor has network authority

Functions.Name: AddComponent
Functions.Description:
Creates a new component and assigns ownership to the Actor this is
	  called for. Automatic attachment causes the first component created to
	  become the root, and all subsequent components to be attached under that
	  root. When bManualAttachment is set, automatic attachment is
	  skipped and it is up to the user to attach the resulting component (or
	  set it up as the root) themselves.

Params.Name: TemplateName
Params.Description: The name of the Component Template to use.

Params.Name: bManualAttachment
Params.Description: Whether manual or automatic attachment is to be used

Params.Name: RelativeTransform
Params.Description: The relative transform between the new component and its attach parent (automatic only)

Params.Name: ComponentTemplateContext
Params.Description: Optional UBlueprintGeneratedClass reference to use to find the template in. If null (or not a BPGC), component is sought in this Actor's class

Functions.Name: K2_DestroyComponent
Functions.Description: DEPRECATED - Use Component::DestroyComponent

Functions.Name: K2_AttachRootComponentTo
Functions.Description: Attaches the RootComponent of this Actor to the supplied component, optionally at a named socket. It is not valid to call this on components that are not Registered.

Params.Name: AttachLocationType
Params.Description: Type of attachment, AbsoluteWorld to keep its world position, RelativeOffset to keep the object's relative offset and SnapTo to snap to the new parent.

Functions.Name: K2_AttachToComponent
Functions.Description: Attaches the RootComponent of this Actor to the supplied component, optionally at a named socket. It is not valid to call this on components that are not Registered.

Params.Name: Parent
Params.Description: Parent to attach to.

Params.Name: SocketName
Params.Description: Optional socket to attach to on the parent.

Params.Name: bWeldSimulatedBodies
Params.Description: Whether to weld together simulated physics bodies.

Functions.Name: K2_AttachRootComponentToActor
Functions.Description: Attaches the RootComponent of this Actor to the supplied component, optionally at a named socket. It is not valid to call this on components that are not Registered.

Params.Name: AttachLocationType
Params.Description: Type of attachment, AbsoluteWorld to keep its world position, RelativeOffset to keep the object's relative offset and SnapTo to snap to the new parent.

Functions.Name: K2_AttachToActor
Functions.Description: Attaches the RootComponent of this Actor to the supplied component, optionally at a named socket. It is not valid to call this on components that are not Registered.

Params.Name: ParentActor
Params.Description: Actor to attach this actor's RootComponent to

Params.Name: SocketName
Params.Description: Socket name to attach to, if any

Params.Name: LocationRule
Params.Description: How to handle translation when attaching.

Params.Name: RotationRule
Params.Description: How to handle rotation when attaching.

Params.Name: ScaleRule
Params.Description: How to handle scale when attaching.

Params.Name: bWeldSimulatedBodies
Params.Description: Whether to weld together simulated physics bodies.

Functions.Name: SnapRootComponentTo
Functions.Description:
Snap the RootComponent of this Actor to the supplied Actor's root component, optionally at a named socket. It is not valid to call this on components that are not Registered.
	   If InSocketName == NAME_None, it will attach to origin of the InParentActor.

Functions.Name: DetachRootComponentFromParent
Functions.Description: Detaches the RootComponent of this Actor from any SceneComponent it is currently attached to.

Functions.Name: K2_DetachFromActor
Functions.Description: Detaches the RootComponent of this Actor from any SceneComponent it is currently attached to.

Params.Name: LocationRule
Params.Description: How to handle translation when detaching.

Params.Name: RotationRule
Params.Description: How to handle rotation when detaching.

Params.Name: ScaleRule
Params.Description: How to handle scale when detaching.

Functions.Name: ActorHasTag
Functions.Description: See if this actor contains the supplied tag

Functions.Name: GetActorTimeDilation
Functions.Description:
Get CustomTimeDilation - this can be used for input control or speed control for slomo.
	  We don't want to scale input globally because input can be used for UI, which do not care for TimeDilation.

Functions.Name: AddTickPrerequisiteActor
Functions.Description: Make this actor tick after PrerequisiteActor. This only applies to this actor's tick function; dependencies for owned components must be set up separately if desired.

Functions.Name: AddTickPrerequisiteComponent
Functions.Description: Make this actor tick after PrerequisiteComponent. This only applies to this actor's tick function; dependencies for owned components must be set up separately if desired.

Functions.Name: RemoveTickPrerequisiteActor
Functions.Description: Remove tick dependency on PrerequisiteActor.

Functions.Name: RemoveTickPrerequisiteComponent
Functions.Description: Remove tick dependency on PrerequisiteComponent.

Functions.Name: GetTickableWhenPaused
Functions.Description: Gets whether this actor can tick when paused.

Functions.Name: SetTickableWhenPaused
Functions.Description: Sets whether this actor can tick when paused.

Functions.Name: MakeMIDForMaterial
Functions.Description: Allocate a MID for a given parent material.

Functions.Name: GetGameTimeSinceCreation
Functions.Description: The number of seconds (in game time) since this Actor was created, relative to Get Game Time In Seconds.

Functions.Name: MakeNoise
Functions.Description:
Trigger a noise caused by a given Pawn, at a given location.
	  Note that the NoiseInstigator Pawn MUST have a PawnNoiseEmitterComponent for the noise to be detected by a PawnSensingComponent.
	  Senders of MakeNoise should have an Instigator if they are not pawns, or pass a NoiseInstigator.

Params.Name: Loudness
Params.Description: The relative loudness of this noise. Usual range is 0 (no noise) to 1 (full volume). If MaxRange is used, this scales the max range, otherwise it affects the hearing range specified by the sensor.

Params.Name: NoiseInstigator
Params.Description: Pawn responsible for this noise. Uses the actor's Instigator if NoiseInstigator=NULL

Params.Name: NoiseLocation
Params.Description: Position of noise source. If zero vector, use the actor's location.

Params.Name: MaxRange
Params.Description: Max range at which the sound may be heard. A value of 0 indicates no max range (though perception may have its own range). Loudness scales the range. (Note: not supported for legacy PawnSensingComponent, only for AIPerception)

Params.Name: Tag
Params.Description: Identifier for the noise.

Functions.Name: ReceiveBeginPlay
Functions.Description: Event when play begins for this actor.

Functions.Name: ReceiveAnyDamage
Functions.Description: Event when this actor takes ANY damage

Functions.Name: ReceiveRadialDamage
Functions.Description: Event when this actor takes RADIAL damage

Functions.Name: ReceivePointDamage
Functions.Description: Event when this actor takes POINT damage

Functions.Name: ReceiveTick
Functions.Description: Event called every frame

Functions.Name: ReceiveActorBeginOverlap
Functions.Description:
Event when this actor overlaps another actor, for example a player walking into a trigger.
	 	For events when objects have a blocking collision, for example a player hitting a wall, see 'Hit' events.

Functions.Name: ReceiveActorEndOverlap
Functions.Description: Event when an actor no longer overlaps another actor, and they have separated.

Functions.Name: ReceiveActorBeginCursorOver
Functions.Description: Event when this actor has the mouse moved over it with the clickable interface.

Functions.Name: ReceiveActorEndCursorOver
Functions.Description: Event when this actor has the mouse moved off of it with the clickable interface.

Functions.Name: ReceiveActorOnClicked
Functions.Description: Event when this actor is clicked by the mouse when using the clickable interface.

Functions.Name: ReceiveActorOnReleased
Functions.Description: Event when this actor is under the mouse when left mouse button is released while using the clickable interface.

Functions.Name: ReceiveActorOnInputTouchBegin
Functions.Description: Event when this actor is touched when click events are enabled.

Functions.Name: ReceiveActorOnInputTouchEnd
Functions.Description: Event when this actor is under the finger when untouched when click events are enabled.

Functions.Name: ReceiveActorOnInputTouchEnter
Functions.Description: Event when this actor has a finger moved over it with the clickable interface.

Functions.Name: ReceiveActorOnInputTouchLeave
Functions.Description: Event when this actor has a finger moved off of it with the clickable interface.

Functions.Name: GetOverlappingActors
Functions.Description: Returns list of actors this actor is overlapping (any component overlapping any component). Does not return itself.

Params.Name: OverlappingActors
Params.Description: [out] Returned list of overlapping actors

Params.Name: ClassFilter
Params.Description: [optional] If set, only returns actors of this class or subclasses

Functions.Name: GetOverlappingComponents
Functions.Description: Returns list of components this actor is overlapping.

Functions.Name: ReceiveHit
Functions.Description:
Event when this actor bumps into a blocking object, or blocks another actor that bumps into it.
	  This could happen due to things like Character movement, using Set Location with 'sweep' enabled, or physics simulation.
	  For events when objects overlap (e.g. walking into a trigger) see the 'Overlap' event.

Functions.Name: SetLifeSpan
Functions.Description: Set the lifespan of this actor. When it expires the object will be destroyed. If requested lifespan is 0, the timer is cleared and the actor will not be destroyed.

Functions.Name: GetLifeSpan
Functions.Description: Get the remaining lifespan of this actor. If zero is returned the actor lives forever.

Functions.Name: UserConstructionScript
Functions.Description: Construction script, the place to spawn components and do other setup.

Functions.Name: ReceiveEndPlay
Functions.Description: Event to notify blueprints this actor is about to be deleted.

Functions.Name: SetActorTickEnabled
Functions.Description:
Set this actor's tick functions to be enabled or disabled. Only has an effect if the function is registered
	  This only modifies the tick function on actor itself

Params.Name: bEnabled
Params.Description: Whether it should be enabled or not

Functions.Name: IsActorTickEnabled
Functions.Description: Returns whether this actor has tick enabled or not

Functions.Name: SetActorTickInterval
Functions.Description: Sets the tick interval of this actor's primary tick function. Will not enable a disabled tick function. Takes effect on next tick.

Params.Name: TickInterval
Params.Description: The rate at which this actor should be ticking

Functions.Name: GetActorTickInterval
Functions.Description: Returns the tick interval of this actor's primary tick function

Functions.Name: OnRep_ReplicatedMovement
Functions.Description: ReplicatedMovement struct replication event

Functions.Name: SetOwner
Functions.Description: Set the owner of this Actor, used primarily for network replication.

Params.Name: NewOwner
Params.Description: The Actor whom takes over ownership of this Actor

Functions.Name: GetOwner
Functions.Description: Get the owner of this Actor, used primarily for network replication.

Functions.Name: IsOverlappingActor
Functions.Description: Check whether any component of this Actor is overlapping any component of another Actor.

Params.Name: Other
Params.Description: The other Actor to test against

Return.Description: Whether any component of this Actor is overlapping any component of another Actor.

Functions.Name: SetNetDormancy
Functions.Description: Puts actor in dormant networking state

Functions.Name: FlushNetDormancy
Functions.Description: Forces dormant actor to replicate but doesn't change NetDormancy state (i.e., they will go dormant again if left dormant)

Functions.Name: IsChildActor
Functions.Description: Returns whether this Actor was spawned by a child actor component

Functions.Name: GetAllChildActors
Functions.Description: Returns a list of all child actors, including children of children

Functions.Name: GetParentComponent
Functions.Description: If this Actor was created by a Child Actor Component returns that Child Actor Component

Functions.Name: GetParentActor
Functions.Description: If this Actor was created by a Child Actor Component returns the Actor that owns that Child Actor Component

Functions.Name: K2_TeleportTo
Functions.Description: Teleport this actor to a new location. If the actor doesn't fit exactly at the location specified, tries to slightly move it out of walls and such.

Params.Name: DestLocation
Params.Description: The target destination point

Params.Name: DestRotation
Params.Description: The target rotation at the destination

Return.Description: true if the actor has been successfully moved, or false if it couldn't fit.

Functions.Name: GetLevelName
Functions.Description: Return the ULevel name that this Actor is part of.

Functions.Name: GetAttachParentActor
Functions.Description: Walk up the attachment chain from RootComponent until we encounter a different actor, and return it. If we are not attached to a component in a different actor, returns NULL

Functions.Name: GetAttachParentSocketName
Functions.Description: Walk up the attachment chain from RootComponent until we encounter a different actor, and return the socket name in the component. If we are not attached to a component in a different actor, returns NAME_None

Functions.Name: GetAttachedActors
Functions.Description: Find all Actors which are attached directly to a component in this actor

Functions.Name: SetTickGroup
Functions.Description: Sets the ticking group for this actor.

Params.Name: NewTickGroup
Params.Description: the new value to assign

Functions.Name: CanBeBaseForCharacter
Functions.Description: Return true if the given Pawn can be "based" on this actor (ie walk on it).

Params.Name: Pawn
Params.Description: - The pawn that wants to be based on this actor

Functions.Name: OverrideNotifyAttachMesh
Functions.Description: For Bone Retarget Feature End

Functions.Name: K2_OnBecomeViewTarget
Functions.Description: Event called when this Actor becomes the view target for the given PlayerController.

Functions.Name: K2_OnEndViewTarget
Functions.Description: Event called when this Actor is no longer the view target for the given PlayerController.

Functions.Name: K2_OnReset
Functions.Description: Event called when this Actor is reset to its initial state - used when restarting level without reloading.

Functions.Name: WasRecentlyRendered
Functions.Description:
Returns true if this actor has been rendered "recently", with a tolerance in seconds to define what "recent" means.
	  e.g.: If a tolerance of 0.1 is used, this function will return true only if the actor was rendered in the last 0.1 seconds of game time.

Params.Name: Tolerance
Params.Description: How many seconds ago the actor last render time can be and still count as having been "recently" rendered.

Return.Description: Whether this actor was recently rendered.

Functions.Name: ForceNetRelevant
Functions.Description: Forces this actor to be net relevant if it is not already by default

Functions.Name: ForceNetConsider
Functions.Description: Force actor enter consider list

Functions.Name: ForceNetComponentUpdate
Functions.Description: Force actor's component to be updated to client

Functions.Name: ForceNetUpdate
Functions.Description: Force actor to be updated to clients

Functions.Name: PrestreamTextures
Functions.Description: Calls PrestreamTextures() for all the actor's meshcomponents.

Params.Name: Seconds
Params.Description: - Number of seconds to force all mip-levels to be resident

Params.Name: bEnableStreaming
Params.Description: - Whether to start (true) or stop (false) streaming

Params.Name: CinematicTextureGroups
Params.Description: - Bitfield indicating which texture groups that use extra high-resolution mips

Functions.Name: SetTextureForceResidentFlag
Functions.Description: Calls SetTextureForceResidentFlag() for all the actor's meshcomponents.

Params.Name: bForceMiplevelsToBeResident
Params.Description: Whether textures should be forced to be resident or not.

Functions.Name: GetActorEyesViewPoint
Functions.Description:
Returns the point of view of the actor.
	  Note that this doesn't mean the camera, but the 'eyes' of the actor.
	  For example, for a Pawn, this would define the eye height location,
	  and view rotation (which is different from the pawn rotation which has a zeroed pitch component).
	  A camera first person view will typically use this view point. Most traces (weapon, AI) will be done from this view point.

Params.Name: OutLocation
Params.Description: - location of view point

Params.Name: OutRotation
Params.Description: - view rotation of actor.

Functions.Name: GetComponentByClass
Functions.Description: Script exposed version of FindComponentByClass

Functions.Name: GetComponentsByClass
Functions.Description:
Gets all the components that inherit from the given class.
	Currently returns an array of UActorComponent which must be cast to the correct type.

Functions.Name: GetComponentsByTag
Functions.Description: Gets all the components that inherit from the given class with a given tag.

Functions.Name: IsHiddenEdAtStartup
Functions.Description: Simple accessor to check if the actor is hidden upon editor startup

Return.Description: true if the actor is hidden upon editor startup; false if it is not

Functions.Name: SetIsTemporarilyHiddenInEditor
Functions.Description: Sets whether or not this actor is hidden in the editor for the duration of the current editor session

Params.Name: bIsHidden
Params.Description: True if the actor is hidden

Params.Name: bIncludeParent
Params.Description: - Whether to recurse up child actor hierarchy or not

Return.Description: Whether or not this actor is hidden in the editor for the duration of the current editor session
Returns true if this actor is allowed to be displayed, selected and manipulated by the editor.
Returns true if this actor can EVER be selected in a level in the editor.  Can be overridden by specific actors to make them unselectable.

Delegate.Name: OnTakeAnyDamage
Delegate.Description: Called when the actor is damaged in any way.

Delegate.Name: OnTakePointDamage
Delegate.Description: Called when the actor is damaged by point damage.

Delegate.Name: OnActorBeginOverlap
Delegate.Description:
Called when another actor begins to overlap this actor, for example a player walking into a trigger.
	 	For events when objects have a blocking collision, for example a player hitting a wall, see 'Hit' events.

Delegate.Name: OnActorEndOverlap
Delegate.Description: Called when another actor stops overlapping this actor.

Delegate.Name: OnBeginCursorOver
Delegate.Description: Called when the mouse cursor is moved over this actor if mouse over events are enabled in the player controller.

Delegate.Name: OnEndCursorOver
Delegate.Description: Called when the mouse cursor is moved off this actor if mouse over events are enabled in the player controller.

Delegate.Name: OnClicked
Delegate.Description: Called when the left mouse button is clicked while the mouse is over this actor and click events are enabled in the player controller.

Delegate.Name: OnReleased
Delegate.Description: Called when the left mouse button is released while the mouse is over this actor and click events are enabled in the player controller.

Delegate.Name: OnInputTouchBegin
Delegate.Description: Called when a touch input is received over this actor when touch events are enabled in the player controller.

Delegate.Name: OnInputTouchEnd
Delegate.Description: Called when a touch input is received over this component when touch events are enabled in the player controller.

Delegate.Name: OnInputTouchEnter
Delegate.Description: Called when a finger is moved over this actor when touch over events are enabled in the player controller.

Delegate.Name: OnInputTouchLeave
Delegate.Description: Called when a finger is moved off this actor when touch over events are enabled in the player controller.

Delegate.Name: OnActorHit
Delegate.Description:
Called when this Actor hits (or is hit by) something solid. This could happen due to things like Character movement, using Set Location with 'sweep' enabled, or physics simulation.
	 	For events when objects overlap (e.g. walking into a trigger) see the 'Overlap' event.

Delegate.Name: OnDestroyed
Delegate.Description: Event triggered when the actor is destroyed.

Delegate.Name: OnActorHitNew
Delegate.Description: same as OnActorHit, but will Recivie StartPenetrating Hits

Delegate.Name: OnEndPlay
Delegate.Description: Event triggered when the actor is being removed from a level.
